verdict.rationale: Robust supply-chain confirmations from TSMC, SK Hynix, and Samsung, alongside strong near-term demand signals from AWS, Dell, and Super Micro, provide fundamental support that outweighs negative sentiment drag from Microsoft's spending concerns and U.S.-China regulatory overhangs. The convergence of stable advanced-node supply and hyperscaler/OEM revenue visibility supports a constructive short-term outlook before the next earnings cycle. Geopolitical and sector-rotation risks remain present but are insufficient to offset the breadth of positive operational read-throughs.

impacts.name: TSMC
impacts.summary: TSMC's on-target Q2 sales, surging AI demand, and capacity expansion confirm stable supply for NVIDIA's sole-source advanced GPUs, supporting near-term revenue execution and shipment predictability before the next earnings. Offsetting this are potential foundry price hikes and broader AI-sector sentiment weakness following the Broadcom-driven rotation, which cap the magnitude of the near-term lift.
